content.problemStatement:
Chef has a sequence AAA consisting of NNN positive integers.

He is playing a game with this sequence. The goal of the game is to maximize the greatest common divisor of all elements of the sequence. Chef may perform the following operation an arbitrary number of times (including zero):


Choose a positive integer ddd and a valid index iii such that AiA_iAi​ is divisible by d2d^2d2.
Divide AiA_iAi​ by d2d^2d2.
Choose a valid index jjj (not necessarily different from iii) and multiply AjA_jAj​ by ddd.
Help Chef and find the maximum GCD of all integers in the sequence he could obtain if he plays optimally!
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains a single integer N. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the maximum possible value of the greatest common divisor of all elements of the sequence.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the maximum possible GCD is 1 because the numbers 1 and 25 do not have any common factors. For the second test case, the maximum possible GCD is 6 because the numbers 27, 3, 15, 1024, and 15 can be transformed into multiples of 6 by dividing and multiplying by appropriate factors.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we first need to find the prime factorization of each number in the sequence. Then, for each prime factor, we calculate the minimum power of that prime factor across all numbers in the sequence. The maximum possible GCD will be the product of these minimum powers. We can use a map to store the count of each prime factor for each number and then find the minimum count across all numbers for each prime factor.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N * log(max(Ai))) — where log(max(Ai)) is the time complexity of finding the prime factorization of a number
solution.spaceComplexity: O(T * N) — for storing the prime factorization of each number

code.Python3:
# COMPLETE, RUNNABLE Python3 code
import math
import sys

def maxGCD(A):
    maxGCD = 1
    for i in range(2, int(math.sqrt(max(A))) + 1):
        minPower = float('inf')
        for j in range(len(A)):
            power = 0
            while A[j] % i == 0:
                A[j] //= i
                power += 1
            minPower = min(minPower, power)
        for _ in range(minPower):
            maxGCD *= i
    return maxGCD

T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(input())
    A = list(map(int, input().split()))
    print(maxGCD(A))
